blocks.content: He smiled. The wrong kind of smile — the kind that meant he'd filed something away.

blocks.speaker: Daniel
blocks.content: Aight, Larry. I'll figure it out.
He bumped my shoulder going past. Deliberate. Just enough to be deliberate.
Jonathan was at the end of the hall. He'd seen the whole thing. He waited until I got close.

blocks.speaker: Jonathan
blocks.content: Nah what was that for real?

blocks.speaker: Jonathan
blocks.content: Now he got a reason to run up on you. You know who that is? That's Daniel Snyder.

blocks.speaker: Larry
blocks.content: I wasn't just gonna stand there.

blocks.speaker: Jonathan
blocks.content: Sometimes standing there is the move. First day, first impressions — that stuff sticks no cap.

blocks.prompt: Jonathan said his piece. Now yours.
